rationale: The cited article is invoked in the main text (not just the bibliography) to support a specific claim that brand personality impressions can be updated over time. It is also used to position the literature gap, where the citing paper says research on creating or managing brand personality change is scarce, with Johar et al. as a notable exception. This gives the citation a clear framing and positioning function for the citing paper's contribution, even though the citing paper does not directly reuse the cited paper's inference-route mechanism or methods.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Johar et al. (2005) as prior evidence that brand personality impressions are dynamic and can change over time. It then positions its own work on brand personality creation and management (e.g., spokesperson effects) as a complementary contribution relative to that prior updating-focused work.
